rulings.Case Title: JOANNE O. DENHAM et al VS. DOUGLASS INSULATION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Scotts Company¿S Motion For The Court¿S Appointment Of Dr. Victor Roggli As An Expert Witness To Testify At Trial Pursuant To Evidence Code § 730
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, February 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant The Scotts Company¿s motion to appoint Dr. Victor L. Roggli as an expert witness to testify at trial pursuant to Evidence Code section 730: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Cardone Industries, Inc.¿S Responses To General Order 129 Standard Plaintiff Interrogatories To Defendants
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, February 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel Cardone Industries Inc.¿s responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff interrogatories to defendants is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
